stories.title: Vet teams believe Jackie was already sick before bald eagle fight captured on video near Big Bear nest
stories.summary: Veterinary teams treating Jackie, the Big Bear bald eagle, believe she was already ill before a fight with two younger eagles was captured on video last week. Now in critical condition at the Ojai Raptor Center, Jackie faces severe anemia and kidney inflammation while eating and remaining alert. Tests for lead, rodenticides and zinc are underway, with no metal objects found in X-rays. Further results will determine the next steps as wildlife experts monitor her progress.

stories.title: Pit found by Quebecer confirmed to be a 390-million-years-old meteorite impact crater
stories.summary: A Quebec amateur astronomer’s 2023 Google Maps discovery has been confirmed as a 390-million-year-old meteorite impact crater. The 25-kilometre-wide depression, located 100 kilometres north of Magpie, Que., is one of the largest recent finds of its kind. Researchers plan to present the findings at an upcoming conference in Germany. The find highlights the role of citizen science in uncovering geological history.

stories.title: Woman attacked by brown bear while hiking Alaskan trail
stories.summary: A woman was attacked by a brown bear while hiking in Bear Valley, Alaska, on July 18, 2026, suffering non-life-threatening injuries. The bear, which emerged from dense alder trees, made brief contact with the hiker before moving on. High winds and inclement weather may have contributed to the surprise encounter. The woman was hospitalized and the bear was tracked and monitored by police using a drone.
sources: ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos

stories.title: 'Other Mommy' trailer terrorizes moviegoers ahead of 'The Odyssey'
stories.summary: A trailer for the upcoming film "Other Mommy" has been causing distress among moviegoers ahead of Christopher Nolan's "The Odyssey." The trailer, which features Jessica Chastain in a disturbing conversation with a young girl, played before screenings worldwide over the weekend. Some viewers took to social media to warn others about the trailer's unsettling content. The film is set to release later this year.
sources: ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos

stories.title: Venice Film Festival Reveals 2026 Lineup (Updating Live)
stories.summary: The Venice Film Festival will unveil its 2026 lineup next year, featuring high-profile international titles. The festival opens with Danny Boyle’s biographical drama Ink, starring Guy Pearce as Rupert Murdoch, premiering September 2. Netflix holds U.S. rights to the film, which competes for the Golden Lion. Closing the event is Giovanni Veronesi’s Dio Ride, an out-of-competition Italian film starring Pierfrancesco Favino.

stories.title: Government to cut business rates for pubs, clubs and music venues
stories.summary: Government to slash business rates for pubs, clubs and music venues in England by 20% from April, saving firms around £1,100 each next year. The £100m package, announced by Andy Burnham, will fund the cut through a review of tax relief on less community-focused businesses. Nearly 32,000 venues are expected to benefit, though the largest live music venues are excluded. Details on eligibility will come at the autumn Budget.

stories.title: Daniel Wu on ‘Cold War 1994,’ ‘Twilight of the Warriors’ and Coming Home to Hong Kong: ‘The Craft Still Challenges Me’
stories.summary: Daniel Wu stars in Cold War 1994, the third installment of the prequel series set before Hong Kong’s 1997 handover. The film, part of the highest-grossing Hong Kong productions of 2012 and 2016, marks Wu’s return to the franchise alongside Terrance Lau. Wu received the Best from the East Award at the New York Asian Film Festival for his role. The production, praised for its scale and script, reunites three generations of Hong Kong actors.

stories.title: Seinfeld actor apologises to reality star for 'inappropriate' sketch when she was 17
stories.summary: Jason Alexander, known for his role in Seinfeld, has apologized to reality star Courtney Stodden for an inappropriate 2012 comedy sketch where he simulated a phone signal on her body. The sketch, which aired on Funny or Die, included crude remarks about her age and consent. Stodden, who was 17 at the time, called the content exploitative and shared it online this week. Alexander acknowledged his actions were wrong and offered his sincere apologies.

stories.title: Andy Burnham's plan to collaborate with social media creators
stories.summary: Andy Burnham, now prime minister, plans to use social media more casually and collaborate with online creators to reach younger voters. His team will prioritize spontaneous, less polished content on platforms like X, Instagram, and TikTok. This approach mirrors a trend started under the previous government, which began inviting influencers to government events. The shift reflects a broader move away from traditional media toward digital engagement.

stories.title: Ruiz and Gavi's reward for World Cup win... 150kg of tomatoes
stories.summary: Spain midfielders Fabian Ruiz and Gavi received 150kg of tomatoes combined as a reward for winning the World Cup, a tradition in their hometown of Los Palacios y Villafranca. Ruiz, 85kg, and Gavi, 68.5kg, were honored at a town hall reception Wednesday. Former teammate Jesus Navas, who won the 2010 World Cup, also attended. The town, home to fewer than 40,000 people, celebrates its athletes with this unique tribute.

stories.title: Charity 'heartbroken' as food ruined in break-in
stories.summary: Charity FareShare North East lost 16 tonnes of food—enough for 7,000 meals—after thieves cut power to its Teesside warehouse freezers on Tuesday. The break-in in Middlesbrough destroyed perishables like milk and meat, forcing disposal of damaged supplies. The charity, which redistributes supermarket donations to over 200 groups, said the loss was heartbreaking for its volunteers. Staff discovered ransacked lockers and open freezer doors when arriving for work.

stories.title: cockroach janta party protest
stories.summary: Thousands of protesters gathered in New Delhi on Tuesday, demanding better living conditions and an end to the alleged harassment of cockroach vendors by local authorities. The protest, led by the Cockroach Janta Party, drew an estimated 5,000 people. The demonstration remained largely peaceful, with some minor clashes reported between protesters and police. The outcome of the protest remains to be seen, but it is expected to continue into the evening.

stories.title: Who am I? Guess The Hundred cricket star No 3
stories.summary: A new cricket quiz challenges fans to identify a star player in The Hundred tournament. The game, hosted by BBC Sport, drops clues daily, with correct guesses earning up to five points. Today’s featured cricketer remains unnamed until solved. More quizzes will follow throughout the tournament.

stories.title: Google burning through cash with spiralling AI costs
stories.summary: Google’s parent company Alphabet reported negative free cash flow of $5.9 billion in its latest quarter, the first time in over a decade, as AI infrastructure costs surged. Spending on AI is projected to reach $205 billion this year, up from $190 billion, driven by competition in the tech sector. Revenue rose 23% to $119.8 billion, but stock prices dropped 4% after hours. The company spent $45 billion in Q2, with 60% on servers and 40% on data centers.

stories.title: HNK Rijeka tie 'a great opportunity' for Derry
stories.summary: Derry City face HNK Rijeka in the Conference League second qualifying round after losing 5-3 on aggregate to CSKA Sofia in the Europa League opener. The tie at the Brandywell was halted by supporter clashes, leading to a 20,000 euro fine and a suspended partial stadium closure for two years. Manager Ryan Lynch called the home crowd’s support "phenomenal" despite the defeat. Derry aim to draw in the first leg to level the tie ahead of the return.

stories.title: House Adopts $95 Billion G.O.P. Budget for Iran War
stories.summary: Republican leaders muscled through resistance in their own ranks to approve the budget blueprint for the war and other measures, but its prospects were far dimmer in the Senate.

stories.title: River levels approach all-time low as drought announcement looms
stories.summary: River levels in Wales are nearing record lows as drought conditions intensify, with July rainfall at just 5% of average following prolonged dry weather. Natural Resources Wales is set to announce an official environmental drought for parts of the country later today. The crisis has already damaged crops, stressed livestock, and killed fish due to critically low river flows. Authorities warn the situation will worsen without significant rainfall.

stories.title: House Passes Defense Bill Amid Iran War Divide
stories.summary: Republicans pushed through the $1.15 trillion bill over opposition from Democrats, who said backing it was akin to embracing the war.

stories.title: 'I am still alive': Indian activist on hunger strike for 26 days loses 11kg
stories.summary: Indian activist Sonam Wangchuk, on a 26-day hunger strike protesting exam paper leaks in India, has lost 11kg. The protests have intensified pressure on Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s government, which announced fast-track courts for those responsible. Wangchuk, who ended his strike location presence, remains a symbol for demonstrators demanding systemic reforms. Opposition leaders continue rallying support amid ongoing unrest.

stories.title: Global Oil Price Nears $100 a Barrel as Conflict With Iran Deepens
stories.summary: The Houthis claimed to have attacked two ships in the Red Sea, and Iran said a third caught fire near the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Russia's businesses under strain from Ukraine's attacks on Wildberries
stories.summary: Ukraine’s drone strikes on Wildberries warehouses in Russia have killed eight workers and disrupted a key online marketplace relied on by thousands of small sellers. Two facilities in Elektrostal and Kotovsk were hit, with damages estimated at £750 million. Kyiv says the attacks target logistics supporting Russia’s military supply chain. Hundreds of Russian entrepreneurs now face lost income as the war’s economic strain spreads closer to home.

stories.title: Butterfly city blooms in shadow of office blocks
stories.summary: A mini meadow in Hull city centre has become a haven for ten butterfly species, including the rare small skipper. The project, launched in 2023 near St Stephen’s Shopping Centre, transformed a Land Registry site by allowing grass and wildflowers to grow undisturbed. Wildlife experts say the meadow supports breeding butterflies by providing essential caterpillar food plants. Officials plan to expand the scheme to boost urban biodiversity further.

stories.title: Blocked by censors, China's animal lovers take fight against abuse offline and overseas
stories.summary: China’s censors blocked online outrage over the June killing of a stray dog and her puppies by four boys under 14, citing protection of minors. Authorities ordered social media scrubbed and urged silence, but animal rights activists took the fight offline and overseas, including New York billboards. Thousands still post reactions weeks later despite restrictions. Authorities have not disclosed further action on the case.

stories.title: ServiceNow bets $40 million on Indian banking software specialist to expand its financial services push
stories.summary: ServiceNow invested $40 million in BusinessNext, an Indian banking software firm valued at $700 million, securing a 5% stake. The Noida-based company, serving over 70 banks globally, reported $32 million in revenue last year. ServiceNow’s deal expands its financial services push, leveraging BusinessNext’s AI solutions and global client base. The partnership aims to accelerate growth in overseas markets.

stories.title: Olympic Mountain Glory
stories.summary: NASA’s Earth Observatory captured a striking image on July 23, 2026, showing the rugged Olympic Mountains in northwestern Washington state from the International Space Station. The composite photo highlights the peninsula’s deep river valleys, alpine glaciers, and temperate rainforests surrounded by blue waters and islands. Olympic National Park covers much of the mountainous core, with the rest managed as national forest, state land, or tribal territory. The mountains formed from ancient undersea volcanic activity and sediment deposits between 55 and 15 million years ago.

stories.title: How the Trump Administration’s Nuclear Deal With Saudi Arabia May Work
stories.summary: The Trump administration announced an agreement with Saudi Arabia on a civilian nuclear program. Such deals, known as 123 Agreements, are designed to prevent the development of weapons.

stories.title: Alarm over hospital lung admissions for children in England’s poorest postcodes
stories.summary: Children in England’s poorest areas were 40% more likely to be hospitalized for respiratory infections in 2024/25, with 1,941 admissions per 100,000 compared to 1,380 in wealthier areas. The analysis, based on NHS England data, highlights a stark disparity linked to deprivation. Experts warn air pollution may play a role, though the data doesn’t confirm causes. Local health services are urged to address the gap amid rising concerns.

stories.title: U.S. Military Presence in Jordan Makes It Iranian Target
stories.summary: Relying heavily on U.S. aid and protection, Jordan is unlikely to restrict the American military presence there, despite escalating Iranian airstrikes.

stories.title: Lego’s Donkey Kong arcade machine lets Mario jump endless barrels — Miyamoto is reportedly happy
stories.summary: Lego’s new $200 Donkey Kong arcade machine set lets players guide Mario through endless barrel jumps, though he never reaches the top. The functional set includes a joystick and jump button, with a lever releasing barrels from the classic 1989 Lego design. Shigeru Miyamoto, Mario’s creator, reportedly approves of the nostalgic build. Next, Lego plans to expand its licensed video game sets.

stories.title: Life-changing MS drug to help people walk now on NHS in England
stories.summary: Fampridine, the first drug to help some people with multiple sclerosis walk, is now available on the NHS in England. Up to 5,000 people could benefit annually. Clinical trials showed about 40% of users improved walking speed and endurance. The drug, already available in other UK nations, boosts nerve signals impaired by MS.

stories.title: Clarity Act Mired In Debate Over Whether to Bar President From Selling Crypto
stories.summary: Democrats and Republicans are haggling about the Clarity Act, a major bill pushed by the crypto industry, as it moves closer to a Senate vote.

stories.title: Teenager drops social media addiction lawsuit against Meta
stories.summary: A Florida teenager dropped his lawsuit against Meta, claiming social media addiction harmed him, just days before his case was set to go before a Los Angeles jury. The 15-year-old, known as R.K.C., had previously settled similar claims against TikTok, Snapchat, and YouTube. His lawyers cited concerns over a lengthy trial and said he wanted to focus on recovery. Meta called the claims baseless and said it would continue defending against such lawsuits.
